_description: Configuration for the title-research skill. Edit this file to add nicknames, dial variant keywords, or adjust detection thresholds.

nickname_lookup.notes: Specific to Submariner refs with green bezel + black dial (e.g., 16610LV, 126610LV)
Specific to 116610LV (green bezel + green dial). Discontinued.
White/silver dial with black subdials on a chronograph
Black dial with white subdials on a chronograph

dial_variant_filter_notes: These keywords describe dial colors/finishes. If the input dial_color does not match, remove the keyword. Special cases: 'White' is often a synonym for 'Opaline' on Tudor GMT refs. 'Blue' may refer to a bezel color rather than a dial color — only filter if the context clearly refers to a dial variant, not the bezel. When in doubt about 'Blue' or 'Green', check whether the word appears alongside 'dial' in the source title.

anomaly_detection.price_threshold_note: Exclude any sold comp where the price is more than this percentage below the median of the remaining results. 40% catches obvious outliers (counterfeits, listing errors, incomplete transactions) without filtering legitimate discounted sales.

reference_placement.chrono24: Reference Number field (dedicated field in listing form)
reference_placement.facebook: Body text, first line after title
reference_placement.value_your_watch: Separate reference field in listing form
reference_placement.reddit: Body text in post, not in [WTS] title
reference_placement.instagram: Not included (no reference numbers on IG)

ebay.notes: Cassini reads left-to-right. Most important keywords first.

chrono24.notes: No hard limit on title field, but concise titles perform better in search.

facebook.notes: No character limit. Title is the first line of the post.

value_your_watch.notes: Title + short catchy description both affect search.

reddit.notes: Format: [WTS] Brand Collection Feature – Size Material. Reference in body.

grailzee.notes: Grailzee auto-generates titles from their database fields.
